channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news and analysis from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: US House Forces Vote on Ukraine Aid, Defying Republican Leadership
item.description: A discharge petition reached 218 signatures to force a floor vote on Ukraine security aid and new Russia sanctions, bypassing Republican leadership opposition.

item.title: Latvian Prime Minister Resigns Over Ukrainian Drone Incident Handling
item.description: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after controversy over stray Ukrainian drones, triggering coalition collapse months before October elections.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Confirmed as Federal Reserve Chair Amid Inflation Crisis
item.description: Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as Fed chair in a 54-45 vote, the most partisan ever for a Fed chair, as oil-driven inflation intensifies from the Iran war.

item.title: IMF Team to Review Ukraine's $8.1 Billion Loan Program
item.description: International Monetary Fund staff will visit Ukraine in coming weeks to assess reform progress and tax base expansion under the wartime loan program.

item.title: Lebanon Demands Israeli Ceasefire at Washington Talks
item.description: Lebanese officials are pressing for an Israeli ceasefire in Washington talks as the US-brokered truce falters and April 17 ceasefire nears expiration.

item.title: Yemen government and Houthis exchange over 1,600 prisoners in largest swap of conflict
item.description: The internationally recognized Yemeni government and Iran-aligned Houthis agreed to exchange more than 1,600 prisoners in the largest prisoner swap of the years-long conflict.

item.title: Gaza-bound aid flotilla departs from Turkey with dozens of ships
item.description: An activist flotilla carrying aid to Gaza departed from southwestern Turkey. The organizers said dozens of ships are participating in the effort to break the blockade.

item.title: Latvia prime minister resigns after coalition rift over stray Ukrainian drones
item.description: Latvian PM Evika Silina resigned after a key coalition party withdrew support over her sacking of a minister following an incident involving stray Ukrainian drones.

item.title: German intelligence agencies drop US-owned Palantir for French alternative
item.description: Germany's domestic intelligence agency chose a French data analysis system over Palantir, reflecting concerns about US-owned software in sensitive government operations.

item.title: China's Xi Hails "New Positioning" in Ties With US
item.description: President Xi Jinping proclaimed a recalibrated US-China relationship envisioning cooperation with measured competition, following talks with President Trump in Beijing.

item.title: Lebanon Presses Israel for Ceasefire in Washington Talks
item.description: A senior Lebanese official said Beirut will demand Israel cease fire at face-to-face talks in Washington as Hezbollah and Israel continue trading blows despite a US-backed truce.

item.title: India Condemns Attack That Sank Indian-Flagged Ship Off Oman
item.description: India's foreign ministry called an attack that sank an Indian-flagged vessel off Oman's coast "unacceptable" as maritime threats escalate near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah Ceasefire Under Strain as Both Sides Report Casualties
item.description: The US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is fraying as both sides report ongoing clashes and casualties, with the truce set to expire soon.

item.title: Iran Allows Chinese Vessels Transit Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran is permitting Chinese vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing attacks on shipping, potentially signaling Tehran's willingness to protect key economic partners.

item.title: China's Yuan Hits 3-Year High as Trump-Xi Summit Begins
item.description: China's yuan strengthened to a three-year high against the dollar while stocks dipped as President Trump and President Xi began their high-stakes summit in Beijing.

item.title: US Asks China to Press Iran on Gulf Attacks, Rubio Says
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US wants China to pressure Iran to change course in the Gulf as attacks on shipping continue and oil prices surge.

item.title: Iraq Sought IMF Financial Assistance Amid Iran War Fallout
item.description: Iraq approached the International Monetary Fund for financial assistance as the Iran war creates economic pressure on neighboring countries.

item.title: UK Health Secretary Resigns Amid Growing Calls for Prime Minister to Quit
item.description: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned as pressure mounts on the British prime minister amid political turmoil.

item.title: Turkish Central Bank Raises Year-End Inflation Target, Warns of War Risks
item.description: Turkey's central bank increased its year-end inflation forecast and warned of risks from the ongoing Iran war affecting regional economies.

item.title: Latvian Armed Forces Deploy Natrix Unmanned Ground Vehicles
item.description: Latvia's armed forces have begun operating Natrix unmanned ground vehicles as part of modernization efforts amid regional security concerns.

item.title: Trump-Xi Summit Focuses on Hormuz and Israel-Lebanon Peace Talks
item.description: President Trump and China's Xi Jinping agreed the Strait of Hormuz "must remain open" during Beijing talks, while Israel-Lebanon peace negotiations resumed in Washington.

item.title: Ukraine's Anti-Corruption Court Places Zelenskiy's Former Chief of Staff Under House Arrest
item.description: Ukraine's anti-corruption court placed Andriy Yermak, former chief of staff to President Zelenskiy, under house arrest as part of a corruption investigation.

item.title: Kyiv Under Massive Russian Drone Attack as Ukraine Suffers Heaviest Barrage of War
item.description: Russia launched hundreds of drones at Ukraine in the third straight day of widespread attacks, with Kyiv suffering its heaviest drone barrage of the war.

item.title: Seven Dead, Scores Missing in Kyiv After Russian Drone Hits Apartment Building
item.description: At least seven people died and dozens remain missing after a Russian drone struck a residential apartment building in Kyiv during the largest drone attack of the war.

item.title: Xi Warns Trump That Mishandling Taiwan Could Spark Conflict
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned President Trump that disagreement over Taiwan could send relations down a dangerous path and even lead to conflict during their Beijing summit.
